title: Good Label and Package Practices Guide for Non-prescription Drugs and Natural Health Products - Canada.ca
content:
## Good Label and Package Practices Guide for Non-prescription Drugs and Natural Health Products
From [Marketed Health Products Directorate, Health Products and Foods Branch and Institute for Safe Medication Practices Canada]
This best practices guide provides direction to industry on the design of safe and clear health product labels. It applies to the following products:
- Non-prescription drugs
- Natural health products
- 1 Overview of the Guide 1.1 Objective 1.2 Introduction 1.3 Scope 1.4 Content contributing to guide development
- 1.4 Content contributing to guide development
- 2 Designing Labels and Packages for Safety 2.1 Introduction 2.2 Planning the design of labels and packages 2.3 Design and layout 2.3.1 Type style and size 2.3.2 Proximity and compatibility of information on the    principal display panel 2.3.3 White space 2.3.4 Colour and contrast 2.3.5 Use of abbreviations, symbols, and dose    designations 2.3.6 Bilingual labelling 2.3.7 Logo, branding, and trade dress 2.3.8 Permanence 2.4 Label information 2.4.1 Key elements on the principal display panel 2.4.2 Expression of strength 2.4.3 Warnings 2.4.4 Expiry date 2.4.5 Lot or batch number 2.4.6 Automated identification (e.g., bar coding) 2.5 Packaging 2.5.1 General packaging considerations 2.5.2 Small containers and small-volume containers 2.5.3 Pediatric products 2.5.4 Blister packaging 2.5.5 Transdermal patches
